preamble: The uvular nasal, often an allophonic realization of the velar nasal in certain phonetic environments (such as before back vowels), can undergo complete deletion, particularly in word-initial or onset positions. This process represents a form of lenition, where a consonant is weakened to the point of disappearance.

examples.note: Deletion of the historical onset nasal, which can be realized as a uvular nasal, in words like 'teeth'.
Deletion of the historical onset nasal, which can be realized as a uvular nasal, in words like 'eye'.

sources: Wong, W. S. Y., & Mok, P. P. K. (2022). A phonological analysis of the onset velar nasal merger in Hong Kong Cantonese. Proceedings of the 20th International Congress of Phonetic Sciences (ICPhS 2023), 2, 2901-2905.
